name: Link
description: Styled anchor element for navigation. Supports internal and external links with consistent visual treatment.

external.description: Opens in new tab with noopener noreferrer

asChild.description: Render as child element (polymorphic). Merges link props/classes and composes event handlers onto the single child (e.g. Next.js Link).

when: Navigation links in footers or sidebars
"Forgot password?" or "Sign up" links in forms
External links to documentation or resources
whenNot: Menu items in dropdowns (use Menu component)
Cards that link to detail pages should still use real link semantics (Link or anchor inside Card)
guidelines: Link text should describe the destination, not "click here"
Use external prop for links that open in new tabs
Use subtle variant for secondary/contextual links
Ensure links are distinguishable from regular text
When using asChild, Link composes event handlers with the child instead of overwriting them
accessibility: Link text must be descriptive of the destination
External links should indicate they open in new window
Links must have visible focus indicators
Avoid using links that look like buttons without button semantics

examples.name: Variants
examples.code: Default Subtle Muted

examples.name: Underline Styles
examples.code: Always underlined Underline on hover No underline

examples.name: External Link
examples.description: Link that opens in new tab

examples.name: As Child (Polymorphic)
examples.description: Renders as a custom element while applying Link styles and composing handlers. Useful with Next.js Link for client-side navigation.
examples.code:
<Link asChild variant="subtle" onClick={() => {}}>
  <button type="button" onClick={() => alert('Navigated!')}>
    Polymorphic link as button
  </button>
</Link>

relations.note: Use Button for primary actions, Link for navigation
Links often appear within Text components

propsSummary: external: boolean - opens in new tab
asChild: boolean - polymorphic child rendering with merged styles and composed event handlers
